rulings.Case Title: LOUIS CASTAGNA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. DEFENDANT CONTRA COSTA ELECTRIC, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE STEWART VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Regarding Union Carbide'S Corporation'S Objections Under General Order No. 129
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. On the application of plaintiffs represented by Waters Kraus & Paul for an order to show cause re contempt against defendant Union Carbide Corporation, the court addresses the three questions posed on page 1 of defendant¿s supplemental opposition brief: While it appears that a practice has developed which judicial officers in the past have explicitly or tacitly approved of permitting plaintiffs to seek defendants¿ compliance with section 4 of GO 129 by way of initiating a contempt proceeding, the contempt procedures of CCP 1209 et seq are ill-suited for this purpose and henceforth it is the policy of the asbestos department that all efforts by plaintiffs to seek defendants¿ compliance with section 4 of GO 129 proceed by way of the motion to compel and related procedures set forth in the discovery statutes. Plaintiffs¿ application will be re-characterized as a motion to compel. Because of the apparently undisputed fact that defendant¿s April 15, 2010 responses were very limited in scope, although those responses were not entitled supplemental responses, they will be treated as supplemental responses for the purposes of subsection G of section 4 of GO 129 and thus any plaintiff seeking to compel defendant¿s compliance with any interrogatory which defendant did not change its prior response must show ¿good cause¿ for the request. Plaintiffs have not shown good cause in their application. Because plaintiffs might have relied on past practice permitting the order to show cause re contempt procedure, they may have been misled to their detriment on this issue and will be given leave to file supplemental moving papers no later than September 22, 2010. Defendant may file supplemental opposition papers by October 5, 2010. Plaintiffs may file reply papers no later than October 8, 2010. (To be Continued)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Application Of Plaintiffs Represented By Waters Kraus & Paul For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt Against Defendant Union Carbide Corporation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 10, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Continued from previous page: A hearing will be held on October 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. in department 220. The parties may agree to different dates to facilitate meet and confer efforts and submit those dates to the court in the form of a proposed stipulated order. The court declines to answer the question about the rights of other plaintiffs in the event defendant provides additional or different responses to GO 129 interrogatories. In the present posture that question seeks an unwarranted advisory opinion and is not ripe for adjudication. =(220/HEK)
